name: Mock Fish (Buddhist)
recipeIngredient: 1 large Potato, cooked, peeled and sliced 1/4 inch thick
10 Wood ears, soaked to soften, tough ends removed, cut into slivers
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle potato slices with flour and deep-fry until golden.
- Drain and set aside.
2. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of the oil, reheat, and add onion.
- Stir-fry for 10 seconds.
3. Add snow peas and wood ears. Stir-fry for another 10 seconds.
4. Add salt, sugar, and water. Bring to a rapid boil, stirring constantly,
until peas are just tender crisp.
5. Add reserved fried potato slices, heat through, and serve.
6. Wood ears are a type of mushroom or shelf fungus. When soaked, it has
a crunchy, gelatinous texture with little taste. If you can't find
them, dried mushrooms could be used as a substitute, though they
wouldn't provide the exact same effect. A closer substitute would be
dried jellyfish, but if available, wood ears are also likely to be
sold.
